Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol branch in tomato fruit peel
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the isomerization of naringenin chalcone to naringenin in the flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor for flavonol biosynthesis and thereby promotes accumulation of quercetin and kaempferol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream step; candidate rate-limiting step in tomato fruit peel flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols, mainly rutin; also isoquercitrin and kaempferol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity; associated with potential protection against cardiovascular disease and use in functional foods.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel; flavonols also retained in processed tomato paste after peel-to-paste transfer during processing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression of Petunia chi in tomato increased fruit peel flavonols up to 78-fold, mainly by elevating quercetin glycosides (especially rutin) and also increasing kaempferol glycosides, while strongly depleting naringenin chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a candidate rate-limiting/key control step in tomato peel flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In control tomato, endogenous chi transcript was below detection in peel at all developmental stages tested and undetectable in flesh, but present in young leaves. In pBBC50 transgenics, chi transcript was detectable at low levels in peel at all fruit developmental stages and relatively high in leaves and in green, breaker, and turning flesh.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive transgene expression driven by double CaMV 35S promoter; plants grown in glasshouse (16 h photoperiod, 23°C day/18°C night) and also evaluated under open field trial conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato peel accumulates naringenin chalcone and rutin during fruit development, while endogenous chi transcript is below detection in peel, suggesting CHI limits peel flavonol biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression of Petunia hybrida chi in tomato caused up to 66-fold and 57-fold increases in quercetin and kaempferol aglycones after hydrolysis, and up to 78-fold increase in peel flavonols overall. 3. Nonhydrolyzed extracts showed rutin as the major accumulated quercetin glycoside, with additional isoquercitrin and kaempferol glycosides in transgenics. 4. High-flavonol lines showed severe depletion of naringenin chalcone, consistent with enhanced CHI activity. 5. The high-flavonol phenotype cosegregated with the transgene and was stable through at least four generations and under field conditions. 6. Processed paste retained about 65% of fresh-fruit flavonols and reached up to 1.9 mg flavonols/g dry weight paste.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of Petunia chi in tomato; Southern hybridization for transgene copy number; Northern blot for transcript detection; HPLC profiling of hydrolyzed and nonhydrolyzed flavonoids in peel, flesh, leaves, and processed paste; segregation analysis across generations; field trial phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for nutritional enhancement and functional food development by generating tomato fruit and paste with substantially elevated flavonol content and no major adverse phenotype reported.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No gross vegetative phenotypic differences observed; ripe fruit showed slight dullness in red color tone due to reduced yellow naringenin chalcone in peel; lycopene concentration was not altered; no taste/flavor differences detected in paste.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal experimentally manipulated gene is a heterologous Petunia chi transgene, but the metabolic phenotype was assessed in tomato. Fruit flesh and leaves did not show increased total flavonoids despite constitutive expression. Accumulation of isoquercitrin in highly expressing lines suggests downstream rhamnosyltransferase activity may become limiting for rutin formation.

Title: Overexpression of petunia chalcone isomerase in tomato results in fruit containing increased levels of flavonols
